title: [packaging] podman-docker: kubic package for ubuntu creates weird /usr/bin/docker
body:
### Issue Description

Thank for podman ❤️ 

I'm using podman installed directly within an WSL2 ubuntu 22.04 instance.
The package is installed from the unstable kubic repository.

When installing `podman-docker` the resulting `/usr/bin/docker` looks weird (see below)
```
#!/bin/sh
[ -e /home/abuild/debbuild/BUILDROOT/podman-4.5.0-0ubuntu22.04+obs74.12.amd64/etc/containers/nodocker ] || \
echo "Emulate Docker CLI using podman. Create /home/abuild/debbuild/BUILDROOT/podman-4.5.0-0ubuntu22.04+obs74.12.amd64/etc/containers/nodocker to quiet msg." >&2
exec /home/abuild/debbuild/BUILDROOT/podman-4.5.0-0ubuntu22.04+obs74.12.amd64/usr/bin/podman "$@"
```
There is nothing at `/home/abuild/debbuild/BUILDROOT/podman-4.5.0-0ubuntu22.04+obs74.12.amd64/usr/bin/podman` and I have to patch that file manually to point to `/usr/bin/podman`

I know that the kubic repos are made on a "best effort" basis though.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. On an ubuntu 22.04 WSL instance
2. set up kubic repos as described here https://podman.io/docs/installation#ubuntu
3. `apt install podman`
4. `apt install podman-docker`
5. `cat  /usr/bin/docker`


### Describe the results you received

```
#!/bin/sh
[ -e /home/abuild/debbuild/BUILDROOT/podman-4.5.0-0ubuntu22.04+obs74.12.amd64/etc/containers/nodocker ] || \
echo "Emulate Docker CLI using podman. Create /home/abuild/debbuild/BUILDROOT/podman-4.5.0-0ubuntu22.04+obs74.12.amd64/etc/containers/nodocker to quiet msg." >&2
exec /home/abuild/debbuild/BUILDROOT/podman-4.5.0-0ubuntu22.04+obs74.12.amd64/usr/bin/podman "$@"
```
There is nothing at `/home/abuild/debbuild/BUILDROOT/podman-4.5.0-0ubuntu22.04+obs74.12.amd64/usr/bin/podman` and I have to patch that file manually to point to `/usr/bin/podman`

### Describe the results you expected

Working `/usr/bin/docker` alias

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

podman installed directly inside a WSL2 instance with systemd enabled

comments.body: ya, that one was caused by the new envsubst thing in the Makefile. I'll see what I can do.
For the record, I just tested on a pure ubuntu system and the issue is the same (no surprise there), I'm removing the WSL2 in the title
latest build should fix it. Check it out.
Closing.. please reopen if issue persists.

Thank you for fixing the issue, I'm trying to install the latest build but I'm hitting:
```
The repository 'https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:kubic:libcontainers:unstable/xUbuntu_22.04  Release' no longer has a Release file.
```
And the kubic repo url returns a 404.
When I try to go through the kubic website it seems there no longer is a slash between `repositories` and `devel` in the download url:
- https://build.opensuse.org/package/binaries/devel:kubic:libcontainers:unstable/podman/xUbuntu_22.04 points to https://download.opensuse.org/repositoriesdevel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/unstable/xUbuntu_22.04/ (note the missing slash)

Retried today, it's working ! 👍

Thanks a lot for fixing the issue ! 🙏🙏🙏
